name: agent-experience
description: Use to DESIGN or REVIEW software, docs, SDKs, and repos so AI agents can consume them as a first-class audience — agent experience (AX), the agent-facing analog of UX and DX. Covers agent-readable docs (llms.txt, AGENTS.md, MCP and tool descriptions, RAG-friendly structure), AI and Agent SDK design (streaming, tools, structured output, agent loops, tracing), repo agent-readiness review, and human-versus-agent audience conflicts. The umbrella AX discipline — it routes to agent-readiness to HARDEN a repo, agent-rules to PROMOTE observed failures into rules, and agent-evals to INSTRUMENT an AI product's eval loops. Triggers on 'make our app agent-friendly', 'review our llms.txt for agents', 'design an Agent SDK', 'is this AGENTS.md agent-ready', 'AX review'. Do NOT use for human developer experience (use dx-audit or dx-design), end-user UX (use ux-audit), or to actually scaffold a repo's gates and hooks (use agent-readiness).

inspired_by.name: AGENTS.md
inspired_by.author: Agentic AI Foundation (Linux Foundation)
inspired_by.contribution: Vendor-neutral, hand-curated repo-root contract coding agents read first; hierarchical discovery with closest-file precedence. Grounds repo agent-readiness review.

inspired_by.name: Agent Skills (agentskills.io)
inspired_by.author: Anthropic
inspired_by.contribution: SKILL.md format and progressive disclosure (~100-token metadata at startup, full instructions on trigger); grounds always-loaded vs load-on-demand splits and trigger-description authoring.

inspired_by.name: Model Context Protocol
inspired_by.author: Anthropic / Linux Foundation
inspired_by.contribution: Capabilities-as-API: tools, resources, prompts with typed inputSchema; grounds MCP-as-tool-source for SDKs and MCP/tool descriptions as agent-facing docs.

inspired_by.name: Evaluating AGENTS.md: Are Repository-Level Context Files Helpful for Coding Agents?
inspired_by.author: Mündler et al. (ETH Zürich / LogicStar.ai)
inspired_by.contribution: Empirical: autogenerated context files drop task success ~3% and inflate cost >20%; always-loaded context must be minimal and measured. Grounds the no-/init and minimal-context heuristics.

inspired_by.name: Effective Context Engineering for AI Agents
inspired_by.author: Anthropic
inspired_by.contribution: Token-budget framing: find the smallest set of high-signal tokens that maximize the likelihood of the desired outcome. Grounds the load-budget split and context-budget heuristics.

inspired_by.name: OpenTelemetry Semantic Conventions (GenAI)
inspired_by.author: CNCF / OpenTelemetry
inspired_by.contribution: Canonical GenAI span/attribute names (gen_ai.operation.name, usage tokens, finish reasons, conversation id); grounds tracing-as-default for AI/Agent SDKs.

inspired_by.name: OpenAPI Specification 3.1.0
inspired_by.author: OpenAPI Initiative
inspired_by.contribution: Schema and description fields as agent-facing documentation; reinforces machine-readable contracts over prose.

inspired_by.name: MCP Tool Descriptions Are Smelly!
inspired_by.author: Hasan et al.
inspired_by.contribution: Evidence that targeted, eval-improved tool descriptions raise tool-call success; grounds the trigger-description test and description quality measures.

inspired_by.name: Cloudflare documentation for AI tooling
inspired_by.author: Cloudflare
inspired_by.contribution: Single-source, multiple-rendering pattern for humans and agents; markdown rendering, token budgeting, and retrieval-friendly authoring.

inspired_by.name: Research Report — Effective Documentation Patterns and Practices for DX, AX, and UX
inspired_by.author: Informed Skills research synthesis
inspired_by.contribution: Synthesizes the unique AX documentation requirements and the human-vs-agent conflict set with a shared/modified/unique inheritance model.

metadata.catalog_summary: the umbrella discipline. Owns the AX review heuristics (agent-readable docs, AI/Agent SDK design, repo agent-readiness, human-vs-agent audience conflicts) and routes to the three arms below for the *doing*. It is a distinct discipline that routes *across* top-level skills (see [`docs/adr/0006`](./docs/adr/0006-discipline-front-doors-vs-one-engine-many-surfaces.md)). Installed standalone it is **review-only** — its build/harden/measure routes point at the arms, so install those alongside it for the full discipline. Provenance in [`skills/agent-experience/skill.json`](./skills/agent-experience/skill.json).
